exemplar.title: How Consistent Is Wastewater Based Epidemiology (WBE) in Testing for the Prevalence Of Narcotics in New York City with Other, Traditional Narcotic Habits Data such as Crime Data and Overdose Rates?

detailedExemplarData.title: How Consistent Is Wastewater Based Epidemiology (WBE) in Testing for the Prevalence Of Narcotics in New York City with Other, Traditional Narcotic Habits Data such as Crime Data and Overdose Rates?

strengths: The title and opening paragraph succinctly communicate the topic, geographic scope, and disciplinary lenses, ensuring clarity of focus.
Clear transitions and headings (e.g., to overdose data) maintain coherence and sustain the research focus.
The research question is clearly stated, narrow enough for a 4 000-word study, and directly addresses the relationship under investigation.
weaknesses: The research question is repeated verbatim, introducing redundancyâits wording could be tightened for precision.
Justification for selecting the four specific illicit drugs and particular years/borough scales is only briefly addressed and could be expanded.
The conclusion reiterates data descriptions but lacks a concise, explicit statement that directly answers the research question.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work consistently communicates a clear and appropriate focus (top-band criteria), and the research question is sufficiently narrow. Methodology selection is generally appropriate, though justification is only partial. Minor redundancies and under-justified methodological choices prevent a full 6, so a score of 5 accurately reflects sustained clarity with some room for tighter focus and deeper justification.

strengths: Source selection is mostly relevant and draws on peer-reviewed journals, official epidemiology briefs, and crime datasets.
Technical proceduresâsuch as LC-MS internal-standard preparationâare described accurately, demonstrating solid content knowledge.
Key terminology (e.g., benzoylecgonine, LOQ, deuterated standards) is used correctly and consistently throughout.
weaknesses: Heavy reliance on a single WBE study limits triangulation; additional or more recent data sources would strengthen depth.
Explanations often remain descriptive with limited synthesis between the chemical and geographic/public health dimensions.
Typographical errors (e.g., âwas tewaterâ) and inconsistent unit precision detract from professional rigor.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates clear and appropriate knowledge, using relevant sources and accurate terminology. However, the work is largely descriptive, with partial integration of concepts and occasional imprecision. This aligns with mid-band performance (3â4), so a score of 4 best captures the consistent but not deeply integrated application of knowledge.

strengths: Research draws on three directly relevant bodies of evidence (WBE, overdose data, crime arrests), demonstrating purposeful source selection.
The essay follows a logical, coherent structure, with each analytical section clearly linked back to the research question.
Interdisciplinary synthesis (e.g., correlating Bronx overdose rates with WBE data) shows emerging critical insight and a coherent comparative argument.
weaknesses: Analysis lacks rigorous statistical depthâno normalization (per capita) or significance testingâto fully support comparisons.
Conclusions remain qualitative and partly inconclusive; sharper, data-driven statements would strengthen the argument.
Recommendations for improving data presentation (e.g., combined charts, summary tables) were not implemented, limiting the clarity of synthesis.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall, the work demonstrates substantial critical thinking: research is well chosen, analysis is coherent, and the argument is structured with interdisciplinary insight. While it lacks the incisiveness and complete rigor of top-band work, it falls solidly into the 7â9 band. A score of 8 reflects strong performance with room for deeper analysis and more robust conclusion support.

strengths: The report adheres to academic structure with a clear title page, detailed table of contents, and consistent pagination.
Referencing is systematic and complete, with a comprehensive MLA-style bibliography and consistent in-text citations.
Headings and section labels facilitate navigation and meet academic conventions fully.
weaknesses: Several visuals have resolution, labeling, or caption-citation issues that limit their integration into the narrative.
Bibliography formatting varies (e.g., URLs and access dates) and could be standardized more strictly.
Dense narrative paragraphs in places reduce readability and could be broken up for clarity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets top-band criteria overall: the academic structure and referencing are exemplary, though some visual and formatting inconsistencies remain. This merits the maximum score of 4 in the presentation criterion.

weaknesses: No RPPF was submitted, so there is no evidence of reflections, personal engagement, or intellectual initiative.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: In the absence of the RPPF and any reflective or engagement documentation, the engagement criterion cannot be credited and must be scored 0.

strandSelections.reasoning: The essay follows a logical structureâmethod description, separate analyses, synthesisâand each section links back to the central question. Some evaluative comments on data reliability and measurement error show emerging critical insight, though nuance is limited and interdisciplinary links (chemistry-geography-public health) could be stronger. A solid but not exceptional critical argument places the work mid-band.

A comprehensive bibliography in MLA-style is appended and in-text citations appear consistently. Minor formatting issues (broken URLs, line breaks) are present but do not impede traceability. Because referencing is systematic and complete, full marks in the top presentation band are justified.

The student presents the topic immediately in the title and reiterates it in the opening paragraph, clearly explaining that the paper will compare wastewater-based epidemiology with crime and overdose statistics in New York City. The geographic scope (NYC) and the disciplinary lenses (chemistry and geography) are explicitly stated. Because the focus and purpose are unambiguous and sustained throughout the essay, the work aligns with the top-band descriptor.

The report includes a word-processed title page, a detailed table of contents, and clearly labeled sections and headings that facilitate navigation. Pagination is present. Overall coherence meets academic norms, fulfilling the top presentation band (3-4) with full marks inside that band.

The student demonstrates solid content knowledge: LC-MS procedures, metabolite selection, and borough-level overdose statistics are described accurately and applied to the argument. Yet the explanations often remain descriptive (long method recounting), and synthesis between chemistry and geography is limited. Knowledge is clear but not fully integrated, matching band 3-4.
The RPPF was not supplied; therefore, no reflective evidence is available for assessment, placing the strand at 0.

The student compares prevalence rankings across methods and identifies inconsistencies, but statistical depth is limited (no significance testing or normalization). Conclusions are drawn (e.g., Bronx consistently highest) and supported with data ranges, yet several assertions remain qualitative and the final verdict is labelled âinconclusive.â This is coherent but not incisive, fitting the lower end of 4-6.

The research question â âHow consistent is wastewater-based epidemiology â¦ with traditional narcotic-habits data such as crime data and overdose rates in New York City?â â is stated verbatim, addresses one clear relationship, and is sufficiently narrow for a 4 000-word study. Minor redundancy in wording and the multiplicity of drugs considered introduce a slight broadness, so a mid-band (5 rather than 6) mark is most appropriate.

The three bodies of evidence (WBE study, overdose briefs, crime arrests) are directly relevant to the RQ and mostly credible. The dependence on a single year of WBE data reduces rigour, but overall the majority of research is appropriate and purposeful, aligning with the mid-band (4-6) and meriting 5 marks.
No documentary evidence (e.g., reflections on initiative or proposed future directions) accompanies the submission, so intellectual initiative cannot be credited and the strand receives 0.

The student explains that secondary WBE data from Centazzo et al. will be compared with NYC DOHMH overdose briefs and NYPD arrest statistics, and briefly justifies each sourceâs reliability. However, there is little discussion of why these particular years, indicators, or borough-level scales were chosen, and no mention of alternative analytical techniques or limitations in data triangulation until later sections. The selection is generally appropriate but only partially justified, fitting the 3-4 band.

The bibliography lists peer-reviewed journal articles, government epidemiology briefs and official crime datasets, all clearly relevant to the inquiry. Nevertheless, the analysis leans heavily on a single WBE case study from 2016; additional triangulation (e.g., multiple WBE studies or more recent datasets) is missing. Hence source choice is mostly, but not consistently, strong.
Without the RPPF, personal engagement with the research process cannot be demonstrated; consequently, this strand must be marked 0.

Tables and three figures are inserted; captions are provided, but two images derive from external sources without full in-text citation under the figure, and resolution is sometimes low. Visuals support the narrative but are not always fully integrated or critically interpreted, warranting 3 marks within the 3-4 band.

Key technical termsâbenzoylecgonine, LC-MS, LOQ, deuterated standardsâare used correctly and consistently. Occasional typographical errors ("was tewater", "perspective metabolites") and minor imprecision in units prevent the level of precision expected at the top band, so the strand fits comfortably in the 3-4 range.

annotations.comment: The title succinctly communicates the research topic, geographic scope, and disciplinary lenses, ensuring clarity of focus from the outset.
Providing a precise word count demonstrates adherence to IB formatting conventions and confirms the essay stays within the 4 000-word limit.
The table of contents is comprehensive and well-organized, with clear headings and accurate page references that aid navigation.
The abbreviations list is thorough and consistent, facilitating reader understanding of technical terms throughout the report.
The opening paragraph is dense and could be split into shorter paragraphs to improve readability and thematic clarity.
Multiple instances of 'was tewater' appearâproofread carefully to eliminate OCR or typographical errors for precision.
When introducing wastewater-based epidemiology, include quantitative excretion rates or example calculations to deepen the readerâs technical understanding.
The research question is clear but reiterated twice; refine the wording to avoid redundancy and tighten focus.
Justify the selection of the four illicit drugs more explicitly by linking each to WBE detectability and public-health relevance.
Table 1 should clearly label units (ng/mL) in the header and define 'ND' in a note to avoid reader ambiguity.
When citing NYC DOHMH data, specify publication years in the narrative (e.g., 2000â2015, 2019â2022) to anchor temporal context.
The figure caption lacks an in-text discussion of what the map reveals; add interpretation and rationale for plant selection.
Expand on why the chosen sample preparation techniques are optimal compared to alternatives, not just by citing prior studies.
The methodology description is thorough but lengthy; summarise core steps and move extended details to an appendix for focus.
The clear description of deuterated internal-standard preparation shows sound understanding of LC-MS quality control.
Consider adding a schematic flow chart of the LC-MS procedure to improve visual clarity of the multi-step protocol.
Report calibration curve metrics (e.g., RÂ² values) to demonstrate the reliability and linearity of quantification.
The discussion describes concentration ranges but lacks normalization (e.g., per capita); adding this would strengthen comparability.
The transition to overdose data is well signaled by a clear heading, maintaining coherence in the overall structure.
Figure 2âs axes are unlabeled and resolution is low; improve label clarity and ensure the source is cited directly in the caption.
The correlation drawn between Bronx overdose rates and WBE data shows emerging synthesis; this strengthens the comparative argument.
The long narrative in section 2.1 intertwines data description and evaluation; split into two paragraphs for clarity.
The introduction of crime statistics broadens the interdisciplinary scope, enhancing the geographic and public-health context.
Could a combined bar chart comparing overdose rates and WBE concentration rankings improve synthesis of the two datasets?
Figure 3âs map legend uses letter grades without explanation; include a key defining âA+ (dark green)â and other categories.
The concise summary that the Bronx has the highest activity across all methods reinforces a coherent argument structure.
Present crime arrest rates per 100 000 residents to align metrics with overdose data and WBE population-normalized figures.
Summarise the comparative borough rankings across WBE, overdose, and crime in a table to enhance clarity of the multi-method comparison.
Cite specific references when discussing methodological uncertainties in WBE to support claims and demonstrate rigorous sourcing.
The conclusion reiterates data descriptions but lacks a concise statement addressing the research question; refine to clearly state key outcomes.
The bibliography format varies between list items (some include access dates inline, others separate); standardize to one style (MLA or APA).
Include DOIs for journal articles consistently to improve source traceability and meet high academic referencing standards.
